# Figure description: Figure 2.jpg

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
Regional geological map with mineral prospect locations, tenure boundaries, and VMS (Volcanogenic Massive Sulphide) horizon annotations. It is a colour-coded bedrock geology map overlain with structural and exploration data.

---

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers a portion of the **Murchison region of Western Australia**, centred on the area around **Coe** and **Meekatharra** districts. The map spans approximately:
- **Eastings:** 560,000 to 600,000 (MGA/AMG)
- **Northings:** ~6,955,000 to 7,005,000

Named geographic/prospect locations include:
- **Mt Mulcahy** (north, VMS prospect)
- **Eastmet Gossan** (VMS prospect)
- **AM14** (VMS prospect)
- **Emily Well** (VMS prospect)
- **Redgum Bell** (gold prospect, west)
- **Cuddingwarra** (gold prospect)
- **Coe** (gold prospect)
- **Great Cuchulainn / Tiara** (gold prospects, southeast)

Tenure references include:
- **E/20/742** (Exploration Licence — Zelda tenure)
- **P 20/2095** and **P 20/2096** (Prospecting Licences — Zelda tenure)

---

## Key Content

### Geology (colour-coded units):
| Colour | Unit |
|--------|------|
| Pink | Granitic rocks (dominant, flanking the greenstone belt) |
| Teal/cyan | Gabbro–Yalgowrah Suite |
| Orange/tan | Felsic Volcanic – Wattagee Formation |
| Light yellow-green | Basalt – Wattagee Formation |
| Light blue | Sediments |
| **Bright yellow** | **Felsic to Andesitic Volcanics** (dominant host to VMS horizons) |
| Mid green | Basalt – Meekatharra Formation (dominant greenstone unit) |

### Structural Architecture:
- A prominent **NNW-trending greenstone belt** runs through the centre of the map, dominated by the bright yellow **Felsic to Andesitic Volcanics** flanked by Basalt–Meekatharra Formation greenstones.
- The belt is strongly elongated, narrow (~5–10 km wide), and bounded on both sides by granitic rocks — classic Yilgarn Craton greenstone belt geometry.
- Multiple **curvilinear/anastomosing faults and structural contacts** are visible, particularly within and along the margins of the felsic volcanic corridor.

### VMS Horizons:
- A near-continuous **red dashed line** (VMS horizons) runs sub-parallel to the belt axis for the entire north–south extent of the map (~40+ km), tracing through Mt Mulcahy, Eastmet Gossan, AM14, Emily Well, and beyond.
- These horizons are hosted within the **Felsic to Andesitic Volcanics** (bright yellow unit), consistent with classical VMS stratigraphy.
- The VMS horizon corridor is tightest and most defined in the central portion where the Zelda tenure (E/20/742) is located.

### Tenure:
- The **Zelda tenure** (bold blue outline) covers a compact block centred around Emily Well, encompassing parts of E/20/742 and the two prospecting licences P 20/2095 and P 20/2096.
- A larger, lighter blue outline represents **Enterprise tenure**, covering a broader area to the north and east.

### Gold Prospects:
- Gold prospects (yellow crosses) — Redgum Bell, Cuddingwarra, Coe, Great Cuchulainn/Tiara — are distributed along the margins of the greenstone belt, spatially associated with granitoid–greenstone contacts.

## Research Value
This figure establishes the **regional geological and structural framework** for VMS and gold mineralisation potential along a major Murchison greenstone belt, demonstrating that a near-continuous VMS horizon corridor extends for over 40 km along strike through multiple known prospects (Mt Mulcahy to Emily Well), all hosted within the Felsic to Andesitic Volcanics. It directly answers the question of **where the Zelda tenure (E/20/742) sits within the regional VMS system**, showing that it covers a key segment of the horizon adjacent to the Emily Well VMS prospect. For a research assistant, this map is critical for understanding that the project represents a **district-scale VMS target** within a well-defined felsic volcanic corridor, with additional gold potential along granitoid contacts — a compelling dual-commodity exploration setting typical of the Murchison.
